---
title: Canonical covariance analysis
jupyter: python3
---

### Set up and load data

```{python}

## Import modules
import abct
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from scipy import io
from nilearn import plotting

## Load data
data = io.loadmat("./data/hcp_data.mat")

```

### Run canonical covariance analysis

```{python}

# Unpack data
W = data["W"]
C = data["X"].T @ data["X"]
k = 5
np.random.seed(1)   # Set random seed

# Weighted variant (with degree correction by default)
A_wei, B_wei, U_wei, V_wei, R_wei = abct.canoncov(W, C, k, "weighted")
A_wei = - A_wei
B_wei = - B_wei
U_wei = - U_wei
V_wei = - V_wei

# Binary variant (with degree correction by default)
A_bin, B_bin, U_bin, V_bin, R_bin = abct.canoncov(W, C, k, "binary")

```

### Show maps of weighted and binary canonical coefficients and components

```{python}

parc = data["parc"].ravel() - 1
n_hemi = len(parc) // 2

gii_left = "./data/S1200.L.very_inflated_MSMAll.32k_fs_LR.surf.gii"
ccas = {"Weighted structural canonical coefficient": (A_wei[:, 0], "inferno"),
        "Weighted structural canonical component": (U_wei[:, 0], "inferno"),
        "Weighted functional canonical coefficient": (B_wei[:, 0], "viridis"),
        "Weighted functional canonical component": (V_wei[:, 0], "viridis"),
        "Binary structural canonical coefficient": (A_bin[:, 0], "inferno"),
        "Binary structural canonical component": (U_bin[:, 0], "inferno"),
        "Binary functional canonical coefficient": (B_bin[:, 0], "viridis"),
        "Binary functional canonical component": (V_bin[:, 0], "viridis")}

for i, (name, vals_cmap) in enumerate(ccas.items()):
    vals, cmap = vals_cmap
    plotting.plot_surf(
        surf_mesh=gii_left,
        surf_map=vals[parc][:n_hemi],
        view="lateral",
        title=name,
        cmap=cmap,
        vmin=np.percentile(vals[parc][:n_hemi], 5),
        vmax=np.percentile(vals[parc][:n_hemi], 95),
    )

```

### Visualize scatters of canonical covariances

```{python}

# Weighted canonical covariance analysis
fig = px.scatter(
    x=np.arange(k),
    y=R_wei,
    labels={"x": "Canonical components", "y": "Canonical covariances"},
    title=f"Weighted canonical covariance analysis",
    width=600, height=600,
)
fig.update_layout(title_x=0.5).show()

# Binary canonical covariance analysis
fig = px.scatter(
    x=np.arange(k),
    y=R_bin,
    labels={"x": "Canonical components", "y": "Canonical covariances"},
    title=f"Binary canonical covariance analysis",
    width=600, height=600,
)
fig.update_layout(title_x=0.5).show()

```

### Visualize scatters of canonical components
```{python}

# Scatter plot of structural canonical components
rs = np.corrcoef(U_wei[:, 0], U_bin[:, 0])[0, 1]
rf = np.corrcoef(V_wei[:, 0], V_bin[:, 0])[0, 1]
fig = px.scatter(
    x=U_wei[:, 0],
    y=U_bin[:, 0],
    labels={"x": "Weighted canonical component 1", "y": "Binary canonical component 1"},
    title=f"Canonical components (structural = {rs:.3f}, functional = {rf:.3f})",
    width=600, height=600,
)

# Scatter plot of correlation canonical components
fig = fig.add_scatter(
    x=V_wei[:, 0],
    y=V_bin[:, 0],
    mode="markers",
)
fig.update_layout(title_x=0.5, showlegend=False).show()

```
